prompts.name: workflow_design
prompts.description: Design a GitHub Actions workflow architecture from requirements.

params.name: requirements
params.description: JSON describing the desired CI/CD pipeline (language, build steps, deploy targets, environments)

params.name: existing_workflows
params.description: JSON of existing workflows from scan_workflows output

prompts.template:
Design a GitHub Actions workflow architecture based on the following requirements.

## Requirements
```json
{requirements}
```

## Existing Workflows (if any)
```json
{existing_workflows}
```

Produce:
1. A list of workflow files to create/modify
2. For each workflow: triggers, jobs, environment promotion strategy
3. Reusable workflow extraction opportunities
4. Security recommendations (OIDC, permissions, pinning)

prompts.name: migration_plan
prompts.description: Plan migration from another CI/CD system to GitHub Actions.

params.name: source_config
params.description: JSON from scan_ci_config output describing the source CI configuration

params.name: target_requirements
params.description: Additional requirements for the GitHub Actions target

prompts.template:
Plan a migration from an existing CI/CD system to GitHub Actions.

## Source Configuration
```json
{source_config}
```

## Additional Target Requirements
{target_requirements}

For each source stage/job, provide:
1. The GitHub Actions equivalent (job, reusable workflow, or composite action)
2. Required changes in approach (e.g., different caching, different artifact handling)
3. Secrets and environment variables to configure
4. Any features that have no direct equivalent and need manual handling

Produce a step-by-step migration plan ordered by dependency.

prompts.name: environment_strategy
prompts.description: Design a multi-environment deployment strategy using GitHub Actions.

params.name: environments
params.description: JSON list of target environments with their requirements (approval, auto-deploy, cloud provider)

params.name: repo_context
params.description: JSON with repo info (languages, cloud providers, branch strategy)

prompts.template:
Design a multi-environment deployment strategy for GitHub Actions.

## Environments
```json
{environments}
```

## Repository Context
```json
{repo_context}
```

Produce:
1. Environment promotion flow (e.g., dev → staging → production)
2. GitHub Environment configuration (protection rules, reviewers, wait timers, deployment branches)
3. OIDC configuration per environment (different roles per env)
4. Reusable deployment workflow design (parameterized by environment)
5. Rollback strategy
6. Branch-to-environment mapping
